topic: EXP-278 — §4.2.2 two-targeted-substring patches (3.6× phrasing + residual strawman adjective)
our_argument_summary: EXP-278 applies two narrow find_and_replace patches to §4.2.2 (id=p4-422) to address EXP-253 c1 holes 1 and 2 (issued as ISS-1690 and ISS-1691). Patch A replaces the ambiguous "3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope" / "10.8 nT above the ±3 nT ceiling" wording with explicit-arithmetic phrasing showing |−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6 and naming the metric (the rule's ±3 nT half-width). Patch B drops the residual polemical adjective "the strawman" from "discriminating only against the strawman 0 nT," leaving "discriminating only against zero," which contrasts factually with Chapman 5–25 nT.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — this is a section-prose patch, not a WIN. No code changed; only HTML prose in data/sections.json.
data_sources_used: Quoted live-dashboard text from john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html (verified 2026-04-27 per the surrounding section text); BOU 2017 baseline figure (−10.9 nT) carried through unchanged
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — EXP-278 makes no claims about dome code; it edits our review prose only.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-278 is a section-prose edit (not a WIN). The underlying §4.2.2 argument cites the dome's live-power dashboard (Domain 11) and predictions.html, neither of which is fetched live by our pipeline; the section quotes screenshots/text snapshots already verified upstream.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N/A — section-level critical analysis, not a dome WIN claim.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: N/A — our analysis, not the dome's prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: N/A — our review text, not derived from dome geometry.

patch_verification.patch_a_ambiguity_resolved: Yes. Old phrasing supported two readings (3.6× the half-width vs 3.6 envelope-widths past the edge); new phrasing names "the rule's ±3 nT half-width" and shows the division explicitly, leaving only the magnitude-multiplier reading.
patch_verification.patch_a_collateral_changes_after_landing: Decider self-applied a follow-on minor patch (ISS-1741, fixed 2026-04-30T05:23Z) replacing "acceptance window" with "falsification window" inside the same parenthetical. Verified in current sections.json: "acceptance window" no longer appears in §4.2.2; "falsification window" is present. This independently resolves SEC-4.2.2 c4 hole 1 (terminology inversion) without contaminating EXP-278.
patch_verification.patch_b_residual_strawman_count: Zero pejorative "strawman" mentions remain in §4.2.2. Surviving "strawman" tokens elsewhere in part4/part5/part8/part8b are quoting the dome author's own "anti-strawman rule" / "Strawman Control" page (meta-references), which are appropriate.
patch_verification.patch_b_grammar_check: "discriminating only against zero, not against the 5–25 nT…" reads cleanly with parallel structure intact.

holes_found.description: OBSERVATIONAL (not blocking): EXP-278 fixes the ambiguity at the inner-parenthetical site but leaves the broader sentence (the BOU-2017 trilemma) as a 7-clause structure with one nested parenthetical. SEC-4.2.2 c4 hole 2 already noted this and recommended em-dash reflow; that recommendation was not adopted by EXP-278 (out of scope — EXP-278 is targeted-substring-only), and remains a deferred polish item. The c4 hole 2 remains live in the tracker.
holes_found.recommendation: No action against EXP-278 itself. The em-dash reflow is appropriate for a future copy-edit pass when someone is touching the trilemma sentence holistically; do not bundle it into a substring-patch.

holes_found.description: OBSERVATIONAL: EXP-278's rationale claims patch A leaves "acceptance window" in place (the phrasing in patch.after literally contains "acceptance window"). On its own, EXP-278 would have left c4 hole 1 (the acceptance/falsification inversion) untouched. ISS-1741 corrected this independently 2026-04-30; the union of integrated patches is now consistent. If this had not happened, EXP-278 alone would have shipped a still-inverted phrase. Workflow note for the decider: when applying overlapping copy-edits, prefer to bundle related terminology fixes into one EXP rather than rely on a follow-up patch landing in time.
holes_found.recommendation: No content change needed. If anyone re-edits this sentence in the future, prefer a single prose pass rather than serial substring patches that leave gaps for the next agent to clean up.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-253 c1 holes 1 and 2 were genuine: "3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope" was ambiguous, and "strawman" did impute deliberate misframing. Both were minor-to-moderate copy-edits, neither a load-bearing argumentative weakness. EXP-278 does exactly what the original c1 review recommended.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — EXP-278 strengthens our review by removing two defender footholds. Nothing about the dome's eclipse argument is rescued by these edits; they are purely stylistic improvements to our presentation.

cross_win_consistency.strawman_cross_page_check: Searched part1–part10 for "strawman". Only meta-references to the dome's own "anti-strawman rule" / "Strawman Control" page remain (part4 ×1, part5 ×1, part8 ×11, part8b ×1). No leftover pejorative use targeting the eclipse claim.
cross_win_consistency.bou_2017_arithmetic_consistency: BOU 2017 = −10.9 nT is cited consistently. The 3.6× ratio (|−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6) is now stated explicitly; no other section restates this ratio with a different (potentially conflicting) figure. Sanity check: 10.9/3 = 3.6333… ≈ 3.6 ✓.

advocate_mode: A determined dome defender attacking EXP-278 itself would have very little to grip onto. Best they can do: (a) argue "3.6× the half-width" still has the same ambiguity since "3.6×" is a multiplier not a count of widths — but the explicit "|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6" arithmetic makes the multiplier reading the only consistent one; (b) argue dropping "strawman" concedes the dome's framing was honest after all — but EXP-278 retains the substantive critique ("the published falsification rule treats zero, not 5–25 nT") which stands on its own. Neither line is persuasive. The patches are net-positive for the review.

summary_for_decider: EXP-278 cleanly addresses ISS-1690 (3.6× phrasing ambiguity) and ISS-1691 (residual strawman adjective) with two narrow substring patches. Both before-strings absent and after-strings present in current sections.json. Decider also self-applied ISS-1741 (acceptance→falsification window) on 2026-04-30, which independently resolves SEC-4.2.2 c4 hole 1. Two minor observational notes only — both about workflow (related terminology fixes ideally bundled into one EXP rather than serial patches). No verdict change. Recommend pop queue_id=288 and close.
